content:
# Pore Geometry

**Summary**: The physical shape and size of the pores within a rock.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2013-geri|Geri, 2013]] investigated how pore geometry affects foam stability and mobility reduction.

- [[2015-adebayo|Adebayo, 2015]] investigated how pore geometry affects the cementation factor 'm'.

## Scientific Context

- [[2013-geri|Geri, 2013]] Pore geometry influences the formation of lamellae and foam stability.

- [[2015-adebayo|Adebayo, 2015]] investigated how pore geometry affects the cementation factor 'm'.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[2013-geri|Geri, 2013]] analyzed via core samples and petrophysical analysis.

## Key Findings Across Sources

- Foam in porous media is characterized by the distribution of pore size and pore throat. ([[2013-geri|Geri, 2013, p. 47]])


- Cementation factor 'm' varies greatly in carbonate rocks primarily due to the heterogeneity of the pore structures. ([[2015-adebayo|Adebayo, 2015, p. 42]])
